message.content:
Nice idea. Here are gift ideas for a hip-hop fan across different interests and budgets.

Vinyl, CDs, and collectibles
- Colored/anniversary vinyl of a favorite album (Illmatic, Enter the Wu-Tang, The Low End Theory, etc.)
- Reissue box sets or deluxe editions
- Framed album art print or a poster from a classic hip-hop era
- Autographed merch or a limited-edition item from their favorite artist (if you can swing it)
- A quality vinyl cleaning kit and a sturdy record shelf or crate

Listening gear and gear for making music
- Quality headphones (e.g., Audio-Technica M50x or Beyerdynamic DT 770 Pro)
- Portable or desktop speaker for friends’ gatherings (Bluetooth speaker with good bass)
- Entry-level MIDI controller (AKAI MPK Mini Mk3 or Novation Launchkey Mini)
- Portable audio interface (Focusrite Scarlett Solo)
- Drum machine or groove box (Korg Volca Beats or similar)
- Turntable and accessories: a basic belt-drive turntable, slipmats, a record weight, and a brush
- Splice or Looperman subscription for loops/samples (great for beat makers)

Live experiences
- Concert tickets to see their favorite artist or a hip-hop festival
- Studio visit or a behind-the-scenes tour with a local artist
- Hip-hop dance class or graffiti/urban art workshop

Books, documentaries, and education
- Biographies or memoirs: “Decoded” (Jay-Z), “The Tao of Wu” (RZA), “Can't Stop Won't Stop” (Jeff Chang)
- Hip-hop history/math: “The Rap Year Book” by Shea Serrano
- Graphic novels or comics about hip-hop culture (hip-hop history or artist-focused)
- Documentaries on streaming or a physical box set (Beats, Rhymes & Life, etc.)

Fashion and home décor
- Hoodie or T-shirt featuring iconic album art or lyrics
- Custom lyric posters or album-art wall art
- Hip-hop-inspired accessories (caps, beanies, rad socks)

Subscriptions and digital gifts
- Vinyl Me, Please or another vinyl club membership
- Streaming gift cards (Spotify, Apple Music, Tidal) or family plans
- Digital gift cards for music gear stores (Sweetwater, Guitar Center) or for buying samples and plugins

DIY and personal touches
- Create a personalized “hip-hop mixtape” or Spotify playlist with a nice cover and a printed liner note
- Customized USB mixtape with a curated set of tracks, remixes, and a note

If you share their budget, preferred era or artists, and whether they’re more into listening, producing, or collecting, I can tailor a tighter list with exact items and price ranges.
